items.text: Revenue from end customers headquartered in China represented about 20% of our revenue in 2025, while revenue from products shipped into China represented about 50% of our revenue in 2025.
items.rationale: Provides specific quantitative detail on China revenue exposure (20% end customers, 50% shipped), expanding on the existing geographic/customer concentration risk with concrete figures rather than introducing a wholly new risk.

items.text: The semiconductor industry has recently been the focus of increased regulatory activity and scrutiny, which has contributed to variability in global trade conditions and supply chains.
items.rationale: This is a vague, generic statement about increased regulatory scrutiny in the semiconductor industry contributing to 'variability' without specifying any concrete new risk or impact.

items.text: Further, certain key materials used in semiconductor manufacturing are primarily sourced from limited geographies.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new risk factor: reliance on key manufacturing materials sourced primarily from limited geographic regions, a concrete supply chain concentration risk not previously disclosed.

items.text: Governments have adopted or proposed measures, including export controls on certain minerals, materials and equipment, that could adversely affect equipment and material availability, cost or movement.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new risk regarding government export controls on minerals, materials, and equipment that could materially affect availability, cost, or movement of supply inputs.
